mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day return frequency falls below 50% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year out-of-sample backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand expansion, which drives corporate earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio or positive return frequency below 45% in rolling 3-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistical significance with p-value greater than 0.10 during early-cycle phases or Rank IC falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPI increase exceeding 0.4 percent signals accelerating inflation that forces markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in greater than 60% of high-inflation trigger months over a 10-year historical sample.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, supporting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces a negative information coefficient or fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth rates reflect stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate revenue growth, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested rolling windows or forward returns are negative in greater than 55% of high-GDP quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the industrial production index exceeding 0.5 percent indicates a sharp contraction in manufacturing activity, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in greater than 50% of triggered instances or the signal loses predictive power during contraction phases.
